verdict_summary: All 4 prior blockers are resolved in the revised spec. However, 3 new blocking defects were found: (1) AC-05 has an internal contradiction — top-level check.expected_exit_code is 0 but execute-push.py necessarily produces exit 1 (grant-not-found path) and the post_check json_field_equals is inapplicable to execute-push.py which writes no JSON; (2) context-dev-20260527-063758-T4.json omits commands/push.md from all five machine-readable file lists (files_to_modify, scope_boundary.in_user_path, requirement.where, root_cause_analysis.affected_files, requirement.scope.included), directly contradicting the ticket Must Have which explicitly requires push.md changes — a dev reading only the context JSON will miss push.md; (3) AC-08 companion_negative_check.pattern is a broken grep BRE expression that causes grep to exit 2 with 'Invalid content of \{\}', making the verifier unrunnable as written (spec_text_vs_execution_drift).

BLOCK-01.original_finding: commit.md prose lines 83-84 abort on CLAUDE_SESSION_ID only — not in scope
BLOCK-01.evidence: Ticket Must Have line 74 now explicitly scopes lines 83-84. AC-07 added with negative check (CLAUDE_SESSION_ID not set must have 0 matches) and companion positive check (CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID min 2 matches). The negative pattern 'CLAUDE_SESSION_ID not set' correctly matches both line 83 and line 84 abort strings as verified by grep (2 current matches, expected 0 after fix).

BLOCK-02.original_finding: push.md:123-127 also aborts on CLAUDE_SESSION_ID only — BA's stated goal of unblocking /push not met
BLOCK-02.evidence: Ticket Must Have line 75 now explicitly scopes push.md lines 123-127. AC-08 added with positive check (CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID in push.md min 1 match) and companion negative check (bare SESSION_ID assignment must be absent). Note: a new blocking defect was introduced in AC-08 companion_negative_check pattern — see BLOCK-03-NEW.

BLOCK-03.original_finding: AC-03 THEN is runtime behavioral claim but check.tool was source_read — spec_text_vs_execution_drift
BLOCK-03.evidence: AC-03 in acceptance-criteria JSON now uses check.tool: subprocess with args [--repo-hash, --branch, --remote, --request-id], env {CLAUDE_SESSION_ID: empty, CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID: test-sid-ac03}, expected_exit_code: 1 (grant-not-found path not session-unresolved path), and expected_stderr_contains: test-sid-ac03. The note explicitly distinguishes exit 1 (grant-not-found = session resolved) from exit 2 (session-unresolved). This is a proper behavioral subprocess test.

BLOCK-04.original_finding: AC-05 and AC-06 say 'three scripts' but applies_to covered only two; execute-push.py missing
BLOCK-04.evidence: Both AC-05 and AC-06 check.applies_to now contain all three scripts: scripts/write-commit-grant.py, scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py, scripts/execute-push.py. Verified in acceptance-criteria JSON.

dimension_results.rationale: All 6 code locations verified by prior QA pass and remain accurate. BA's live observations (which python, which python3, env var inspection) documented at tier_2_verified. No new evidence quality issues.

objections.finding: The ticket Must Have section explicitly requires fixing commands/push.md lines 123-127 and AC-08 was added to verify it. However, the context JSON — the machine-readable artifact dev reads to determine what to implement — omits commands/push.md from all five file lists: requirement.where, requirement.scope.included, root_cause_analysis.affected_files, scope_boundary.in_user_path, and development_approach.files_to_modify. A dev agent reading context JSON as its primary implementation guide will never modify push.md. The ticket and context JSON are inconsistent on scope, and the context JSON will win at execution time.
objections.proposed_fix: Add 'commands/push.md' to: (a) requirement.where[], (b) requirement.scope.included[] with entry 'commands/push.md lines 123-127 — SESSION_ID fallback expression and abort prose update', (c) root_cause_analysis.affected_files[], (d) scope_boundary.in_user_path[], (e) development_approach.files_to_modify[]. Optionally add a component_chain entry for commands/push.md:123-127 documenting its role and the fix.

dimension_results.rationale: BA ran grep on commands/*.md for bare python invocations and grep on scripts/*.py for CLAUDE_SESSION_ID reads. Both searches are documented with results. The localization_evidence field cites specific tool outputs. No investigation deliverable was requested by the user requirement that was skipped. The out-of-scope observation for dev-command.md:695 is correctly handled.

objections.finding: commands/push.md is absent from context JSON's authoritative file lists (requirement.where, scope.included, affected_files, in_user_path, files_to_modify) even though the ticket's Must Have and Technical Hints correctly name it. The file exists and contains the bug at line 123 (SESSION_ID set from CLAUDE_SESSION_ID only, no fallback). The affected_file_accuracy dimension applies in both directions: the inverse failure is files BA should identify but did not include in the machine-readable artifact.
objections.proposed_fix: Same as SA-01-NEW. Update all five context JSON file lists to include commands/push.md.

objections.finding: AC-05 THEN clause says 'sid equals explicit-sid' and applies to all three scripts including execute-push.py. The check.expected_exit_code is 0 and check.post_check is 'json_field_equals: sid == explicit-sid'. However, execute-push.py does not write a JSON file with a sid field — it reads session_id from env and constructs a grant PATH containing session_id as a directory component, then exits 1 when no grant file exists. A verifier executing AC-05 against execute-push.py using the top-level fields will see exit code 1 and declare FAIL on a correctly-implemented fix. The note says 'For execute-push.py: expected_exit_code is 1 not 0; assert stderr contains explicit-sid in grant path' but top-level check.expected_exit_code remains 0 and post_check remains json_field_equals. Notes do not override top-level fields in machine verification. This is spec_text_vs_execution_drift: the written check recipe does not match the expected runtime behavior for execute-push.py.
objections.proposed_fix: Split AC-05 into AC-05a (write-commit-grant.py and write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py — expected_exit_code: 0, post_check: json_field_equals: sid == explicit-sid) and AC-05b (execute-push.py — expected_exit_code: 1, expected_stderr_contains: explicit-sid, confirming grant path was constructed with CLAUDE_SESSION_ID value). Alternatively keep single AC-05 but restructure check as an array of per-script entries each with correct expected_exit_code and assertion type.

objections.finding: AC-08 companion_negative_check.pattern decodes from JSON to: SESSION_ID="\$\{CLAUDE_SESSION_ID\}". Running grep with this pattern causes grep to exit 2 with 'Invalid content of \{\}' because BRE interprets \{CLAUSE_SESSION_ID\} as a malformed interval quantifier. The check is literally unrunnable as written with standard grep. Verified: subprocess.run(['grep', '-c', pattern, 'commands/push.md']) returns exit code 2 and error 'grep: Invalid content of \{\}'. The check.tool is 'grep' with no flag qualifier, which defaults to BRE. This is spec_text_vs_execution_drift: the written check recipe does not execute.
objections.proposed_fix: Option A: Add 'tool_flags': '-F' to the companion_negative_check object and use plain pattern SESSION_ID="${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}" (fixed-string mode, no escaping needed). Option B: Simplify pattern to a grep-safe substring such as 'CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}"' which uniquely identifies the bare non-fallback assignment and works with BRE. Option C: Simplify pattern to 'CLAUDE_SESSION_ID:-' absent (a negative check for the fallback's absent marker). Verify the chosen pattern: (a) matches current broken push.md:123, (b) does NOT match the intended fixed form SESSION_ID="${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID:-${CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID:-}}".

all_objections.summary: Context JSON omits push.md from all five file lists — dev will miss the push.md fix when reading context JSON
Same root cause as BLOCK-01-NEW — push.md omitted from context JSON affected_files despite being in ticket Must Have
AC-05 top-level expected_exit_code: 0 and post_check json_field_equals are wrong for execute-push.py — note override insufficient
AC-08 companion_negative_check grep pattern triggers 'Invalid content of \{\}' — check is unrunnable as written

advisory_observations.finding: AC-01 is purely negative (zero matches for bare python invocations). A deletion of the invocation lines entirely would also produce zero matches. The acceptance-criteria JSON does not include a companion positive check confirming python3 appears at the three expected invocation lines. This was ADV-01 in pass 1 and remains. Recommended: add companion positive check 'commands/commit.md contains python3 /root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py at least once'.

AC-02, AC-04, and AC-05 write grant/sentinel files to /tmp. Tests that write to /tmp leave real-looking grant/sentinel files in the shared directory. Recommend --output-dir $(mktemp -d) with cleanup. This was ADV-02 in pass 1 and remains.

ground_truth_verification.commit_md_line_83_content: If CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is not set, abort immediately with: Cannot write bulk-commit sentinel: CLAUDE_SESSION_ID not set. (verified, contains 'CLAUDE_SESSION_ID not set', no CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID)
ground_truth_verification.commit_md_line_84_content: BULK=false prose with abort: Cannot write commit grant: CLAUDE_SESSION_ID not set. (verified, contains 'CLAUDE_SESSION_ID not set', no CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID)
ground_truth_verification.push_md_line_123_content: SESSION_ID="${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}" (verified, no fallback expression, no CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID)
ground_truth_verification.push_md_line_127_content: Cannot dispatch push-analyst: CLAUDE_SESSION_ID not set. (verified)
ground_truth_verification.execute_push_exit_1_meaning: grant file not found (session resolved, grant path constructed with session_id)

what_ba_got_right_in_pass2: All 4 prior blockers (BLOCK-01 through BLOCK-04) addressed in the revised spec
AC-07 negative check pattern 'CLAUDE_SESSION_ID not set' correctly catches both line 83 and line 84 abort strings (2 current matches confirmed by grep)
AC-07 companion positive check (min 2 CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID) prevents false pass by deletion
AC-08 positive check (CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID min 1 in push.md) correctly verifies primary intent
AC-03 now uses subprocess with exit 1/exit 2 distinction — correct runtime behavioral test
AC-06 execute-push.py inclusion is correct — both vars unset should produce exit 2
Fallback order (CLAUDE_SESSION_ID first, CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID second) is correct in ticket
Backward compatibility requirement (CLAUDE_SESSION_ID priority when set) correctly maintained

required_ba_changes.action: Update context-dev-20260527-063758-T4.json to add 'commands/push.md' to all five file lists: requirement.where, requirement.scope.included (e.g. 'commands/push.md lines 123-127 — SESSION_ID fallback expression and abort prose update'), root_cause_analysis.affected_files, scope_boundary.in_user_path, and development_approach.files_to_modify. Optionally add a component_chain entry for commands/push.md:123-127.

Fix AC-05 in acceptance-criteria-dev-20260527-063758-T4.json to resolve the internal contradiction for execute-push.py. Option A: Split into AC-05a (write-commit-grant.py + write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py: expected_exit_code 0, post_check json_field_equals: sid == explicit-sid) and AC-05b (execute-push.py: expected_exit_code 1, expected_stderr_contains: explicit-sid in grant path). Option B: Restructure AC-05.check as an array of per-script sub-checks each with their own expected_exit_code and assertion. Remove the note-based override pattern — notes do not override top-level fields for machine verification.

Fix AC-08 companion_negative_check.pattern in acceptance-criteria-dev-20260527-063758-T4.json. The current pattern causes grep to exit 2 with 'Invalid content of \{\}'. Use one of: (A) add 'tool_flags': '-F' and use plain pattern SESSION_ID="${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}" as a fixed string; (B) simplify to a grep-safe substring that uniquely identifies the bare assignment, e.g. 'CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}"'. Verify the fix: (a) pattern must match current broken push.md:123, (b) pattern must NOT match the intended fixed form containing the fallback expression.

verification_methods: Read ticket, context JSON, acceptance-criteria JSON, prior QA report
grep commit.md for bare python tokens (3 confirmed at lines 81/89/153)
grep commit.md for CLAUDE_SESSION_ID abort strings (2 confirmed at lines 83/84)
grep push.md for SESSION_ID and CLAUDE_SESSION_ID patterns (bare assignment at line 123 confirmed)
Read execute-push.py lines 100-140 to verify exit codes and grant path construction
Python subprocess test of AC-08 companion_negative_check pattern against grep (exit 2, Invalid content confirmed)
Python test of AC-07 negative pattern against commit.md (2 matches confirmed)
Cross-checked context JSON file lists against ticket Must Have (push.md omission confirmed)
Verified execute-push.py exit 1 semantics at lines 130-137 (grant-not-found path)
Verified AC-05 top-level expected_exit_code: 0 against execute-push.py actual exit 1
